creator: tmclaugh@gmail.com
text:
I've never liked the fact that sudo uses a different ldap config file 
from nss_ldap but I've just left it alone.  ldap.conf is sudo's default 
and I believe nss_ldap's default though it's changed in the port.  I 
just symlink ldap.conf to nss_ldap.conf personally.  Since 1.7 will be 
out eventually and requires ldap setup changes, I think I'll just change 
the port to use nss_ldap.conf.  I can't see a reason why someone would 
use a seperate ldap config for sudo and nss_ldap.

tom
